document.source_title: Israel begins deporting Gaza aid flotilla activists amid global outcry
document.summary: Israel has deported all foreign activists abducted from a Gaza aid flotilla amid international condemnation. The foreign ministry confirmed that the activists were being transported to airports for deportation, with many being sent to Ramon Airport and others to Ben Gurion Airport. The actions of Israeli forces have drawn criticism from multiple countries, leading to diplomatic protests and calls for sanctions against Israeli officials.
key_takeaways: All foreign activists from the Gaza aid flotilla have been deported.
The deportations were prompted by global outcry over the treatment of the activists.
Israel's foreign ministry stated it will not allow breaches of the naval blockade on Gaza.
Ten countries condemned the abductions as violations of international law.
Israeli Minister Ben-Gvir's behavior has drawn significant criticism, including from Prime Minister Netanyahu.
The deportations are reportedly the fastest Israel has ever conducted.
document.why_it_matters: The incident highlights ongoing tensions regarding Israel's treatment of activists and the broader implications for international relations, particularly concerning humanitarian law and the rights of individuals in conflict zones.

claims.claim_text: Israel has deported all foreign activists from the Gaza aid flotilla.
claims.evidence_text: Israel says it has deported all the foreign activists abducted by Israeli forces who stormed a Gaza aid flotilla.

claims.claim_text: The deportations were the fastest Israel has ever carried out.
claims.evidence_text: The ongoing deportations were the fastest Israel has ever carried out, as it scrambles to contain the public relations damage caused by Ben-Gvir’s video.

entities.name: Adalah
entities.description: Legal Center for Arab Minority Rights in Israel.
entities.evidence_text: Earlier, the Legal Center for Arab Minority Rights in Israel, Adalah, told Al Jazeera that most of the roughly 430 abducted activists were being transported to Ramon Airport.

entities.name: Israeli Government
entities.description: The government of Israel, responsible for the actions taken against the activists.
entities.evidence_text: Israel will not permit any breach of the lawful naval blockade on Gaza.

entities.name: Itamar Ben-Gvir
entities.description: Israeli National Security Minister criticized for his behavior towards activists.
entities.evidence_text: The criticism comes after far-right National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir posted a video on X on Wednesday of him taunting activists.

entities.name: Benjamin Netanyahu
entities.description: Prime Minister of Israel who rebuked Ben-Gvir's behavior.
entities.evidence_text: Ben-Gvir’s behaviour prompted a rare rebuke from Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

events.event: Deportation of Gaza aid flotilla activists
events.evidence_text: Israel says it has deported all the foreign activists abducted by Israeli forces who stormed a Gaza aid flotilla.
On Tuesday evening, Israeli forces finished intercepting the last of the more than 50 boats of the Global Sumud Flotilla.

citations.title: Israel begins deporting Gaza aid flotilla activists amid global outcry

source_credibility.reason: Al Jazeera is a well-established news organization known for its coverage of international events.
